title: Logo Misuse Rules

core_principle.statement: Any misuse of the logo directly breaks the architectural engineering system perception of the brand.
core_principle.interpretation: The logo is a structured system artifact, not a decorative or flexible visual element.

critical_misuse_categories.definition: Altering proportions, grid alignment, or structural geometry of the logo.
Using non-AECS colors or incorrect hierarchy in logo rendering.
violations: Replacing engineering colors with decorative palettes
Using gold as primary structure color
critical_misuse_categories.impact: Shifts brand perception from engineering system to decorative brand

critical_misuse_categories.definition: Changing internal logic of logo construction system.
Adding visual effects to improve aesthetic appearance.
critical_misuse_categories.impact: Converts engineering identity into decorative branding

critical_misuse_categories.definition: Replacing system-based geometry with pictorial or illustrative symbols.

gold_accent_rules.strict_rule: Architectural Gold (#B89A63) must only appear at transformation point
gold_accent_rules.risk: Overuse of gold repositions brand as luxury decoration rather than engineering system

grid_violation_rules.definition: Any deviation from 10x10 modular grid system
grid_violation_rules.impact: Breaks perception of structured engineering system

variant_misuse_rules.variant_A: Must remain primary identity; not replaceable with decorative versions
variant_misuse_rules.variant_B: Cannot be used as decorative logo alternative
variant_misuse_rules.variant_C: Restricted to EPC and execution contexts only

context_misuse.definition: Using logo in environments that contradict engineering system positioning

strict_prohibitions: Any visual redesign outside AECS system
Use of non-standard colors or effects

strategic_goal.objective: Preserve strict engineering identity of logo across all applications
strategic_goal.outcome: Maintain consistent perception of AvizSazeh as an Architectural Engineering System Brand
